paper_title: NMR and GC-MS Analysis of Lipid Extracts

methods.description: This method describes the process of using NMR spectroscopy to analyze lipid extracts for quantitative assessment of fatty acids, utilizing the ERETIC method for calibration and peak integration.

steps.description: Crude microalgal extracts were dissolved in 700 µL of CDCl3/CD3OD 1:1 (v/v) and transferred to a 5-millimeter NMR tube.
steps.searchable_summary: Dissolve microalgal extracts in solvent and transfer to NMR tube

steps.description: 1H-NMR spectra were recorded on a Bruker DRX 600 spectrometer equipped with an inverse TCI CryoProbe.
steps.searchable_summary: Record 1H-NMR spectra using Bruker DRX 600 spectrometer

steps.description: Chemical shift was referred to CHD2OD signal at δ 3.34.
steps.searchable_summary: Reference chemical shift to CHD2OD signal

steps.description: Quantitative assessment of fatty acids was established using the ERETIC method.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform quantitative assessment using ERETIC method

steps.description: Peak integration, ERETIC measurements and spectrum calibration were obtained by the specific subroutines of Bruker Top-Spin 3.1 program.
steps.searchable_summary: Integrate peaks and calibrate spectrum using Bruker Top-Spin 3.1

steps.description: The diagnostic peaks in the region between 2.38 and 2.28 ppm, centered at 2.35 ppm, and between 0.99 and 0.95, centered at 0.97 ppm were integrated to assess the µmol of Total Fatty Acids (TFA) and ω-3 fatty acids, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Integrate diagnostic peaks to assess TFA and ω-3 fatty acids
outputs: TFA and ω-3 fatty acids data

methods.description: This method outlines the procedure for determining the total fatty acid composition of lipid extracts using GC-MS analysis of fatty acid methyl esters (FAMEs) after saponification.

steps.description: Saponify lipid extracts with Na2CO3 in methanol at 40°C for 4 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: Saponify lipid extracts with Na2CO3 in methanol

steps.description: Dilute the reaction mixture with milliQ water, neutralize with HCl 1M, and extract with diethylether three times.
steps.searchable_summary: Neutralize and extract saponified mixture with diethylether

steps.description: Dry combined organic extracts under a nitrogen stream and dissolve in MeOH at a final concentration of 1 µg µL−1.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry and dissolve organic extracts in MeOH

steps.description: Analyze using GC-MS equipped with an ion-trap, EI (70 eV), a 5% diphenyl column, an injector temperature of 210°C, a transfer line temperature of 280°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform GC-MS analysis with specified parameters

steps.description: Elution of free fatty acid methyl esters required an increasing gradient of temperature: 160°C for 3 min, increase by 3°C/min up to 260°C, increase by 30°C/min up to 310°C, 7 min at 310°C.
Identify FAMEs by comparing retention time and mass spectra with FAMEs' standard mixture.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify FAMEs by comparing with standards

steps.description: Express fatty acid content as a percentage of total fatty acids.
